capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP navigate_page, click Copy Evidence, take_screenshot

notes: The scene is still a desktop preview of the ED chest-pain station, not a worn-headset clinical scene validation.
This capture is focused on evidence-copy UX; it does not improve or validate actor, hand, or equipment realism.
The Quest Evidence panel visibly distinguishes clipboard success from readiness: Copied: draft; 19 blockers.
The Copy Evidence action updates an adjacent live status while leaving the Validation row visible.
Desktop layout is readable, but this does not prove Full VR panel scale, occlusion, or headset comfort.
The copied payload remains explicitly labeled as a draft with blockers and cannot be mistaken for Quest readiness.

observations.finding: After clicking Copy Evidence, the live status reads: Copied: draft; 19 blockers.
The adjacent Validation row remains explicit: draft only | 19 blockers.

adversarialNotes: This screenshot is desktop-browser evidence only; it does not validate Quest 3 physical frame pacing, headset input latency, in-headset readability, or comfort.
The copied payload is intentionally still a draft because immersiveFramesObserved is 0, immersiveSessionStarted is false, and no headset input, trace, or locomotion evidence is present.
The status reduces the risk that an operator treats a successful clipboard action as a successful readiness capture, but future headset UX should confirm the same status remains readable in Full VR.
